action: Open Notepad on the computer, create a new instance of Notepad (if needed), and type 'Hi Vinitha' in the new Notepad window.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps:
task is to open Notepad and type 'Hi Vinitha' in a new instance. On the desktop, I see the Notepad++ icon (Box ID 13), but the instruction specifies Notepad, which is the default Windows text editor. To ensure I open the correct application, I will use the Windows Start menu to search for and open Notepad.
Action Description: Click the Windows Start button to open the Start menu and search for Notepad.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Windows Start button to open the Start menu and search for Notepad. The current screenshot shows the Start menu open, with a search bar visible at the bottom. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to type 'Notepad' in the search bar to locate and open the default Notepad application.
Action Description: Type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: I typed 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. The current screenshot shows the search results for 'Notepad', with the Notepad application icon and label clearly visible. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to open Notepad by clicking on its icon in the search results.
Action Description: Click the Notepad application icon in the Start menu search results to open Notepad.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Notepad icon in the Start menu search results. The current screenshot shows a Notepad window open, with the typical blank editing area and status bar at the bottom (Ln 1, Col 1, etc.). This confirms Notepad has launched successfully. The next step is to type 'Hi Vinitha' in the new Notepad window. Since the main text area is already focused (as is typical when Notepad opens), I will proceed to type the required text.
Action Description: Type 'Hi Vinitha' in the Notepad window.
